design_philosophy.core_loop: Advance through side-scrolling stages, defeat enemies and bosses using available weapons, progress through film-adapted levels
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (ammo scarcity vs enemy density) combined with navigation hazards and enemy placement
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Embody RoboCop as a powerful law enforcement cyborg clearing dangerous urban environments with firepower and tactical choices
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hybrid run-and-gun with beat-em-up mechanics adapted from arcade tradition; emphasis on weapon variety and stage progression over mechanical depth

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives system typical of NES action games
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression with limited mid-stage checkpoints
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Weapon selection provides tactical choice; limited ability to adjust difficulty directly

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon pickup and exchange system; players collect various weapons beyond the unlimited-ammo Auto-9 starter weapon
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage completion unlocks subsequent levels; weapons discovered through progression and enemy drops
replayability_hooks: Multiple weapon options encourage varied playstyles
High difficulty encourages skill improvement through repetition

world_building_techniques: Stages adapted directly from 1987 film narrative
Digitized voice intermissions featuring original film actors
Environmental stage design reflecting film locations and story progression

danger_signals: Armed enemy gangs throughout urban stages
Escalating threat from OCP corporate interests
environmental_storytelling: OCP corporate presence indicating institutional corruption
Stage layout progression mirrors film plot escalation

sources_used: wikipedia - List of RoboCop video games (RoboCop section)
